Zumaik's Animation  

Description

1: Summon Pet: PCPetEncS11L055Anim

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 350 Skill: Conjuration
Casting Time: 12 Recast Time: 24
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Range: 0
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Deletable: No Blockable: Yes
Focusable: Yes Dispellable: Yes
Interruptable: Yes Target Type: Self
Spell Type: Beneficial Source: Live 12/03

Game Description

Animates a pair of swords to attack your enemies. Consumes a tiny dagger when cast.

Reagents

  • Tiny Dagger x 1

Items with this effect

  • Spell: Zumaik's Animation

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Zumaik's Animation
ClassLevel
ENC55
Expansion:

Duration:
Instant
  • Comments (14)
  • Sold By (1)
  • Dropped By (12)
  • Crafted (1)

The Field of Bone

Merchants:


The Plane of Knowledge
  • Illusionist Lobaen - 83pp 1gp 5sp 7cp
  • Spell: Zumaik`s Animation - Research 171

This spell is crap
QuoteReply
# Oct 04 2021 at 12:06 AM Rating: Decent
Ardnahoy
Scholar
340 posts
So, the level 48 pet spell summons a level 43 pet... and this spell, which is level 55, summons a level 41 pet. Complete junk.
____________________________
Duke Ardnahoy
Noble Lord Protector of the 70th Crusade

This spell is crap
QuoteReply
# Nov 02 2021 at 11:54 PM Rating: Decent
hullan
244 posts
The level 48 animation spell summoned, at lest for me, a level 34 (at character level 48) to a 39 level (at character level 50). They might scale with levels I dunno.
▲ Page top
pet buffing
QuoteReply
# May 18 2007 at 4:50 AM Rating: Decent
lhr
Scholar
50 posts
Nobody has mentioned what buffs this pet can take, so I will add my comments since I find this animation very useful.
Although this pet is lt blue at to me at 55, it is high enough in level to use the better Mage, summoned weapons (I always use Girdle of Magi`kot, Muzzle of Mardu, Blade of the Kedge and/or Blade of Walnan) and it can be buffed with Virtue and Boon of the Garou. Buffed further with haste, strengthen, and Feedback, I can easily solo for aa's in Natimbi and Dranik's Scar. Granted, most of these trash mobs are also lt blue and green, but it's safe, easy and profitable. Also, with a Druid, this pet makes a pretty decent tank for better exp mobs.

Pet level change
QuoteReply
# Dec 03 2003 at 3:47 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591761253141
Scholar
49 posts
Noticed that the level of this pet has changed with a recent patch. Pre-patch, the pet was dk blue to my Enc 56; now it always cons lt. blue to my Enc 56. Tried maxing my CHA and still get only lt. blue pets. Was unable to tell if the DPS dropped as well but suspect it did. Any ideas what happened? This pet now is essentially little better than the level 49 one.

Edited, Wed Dec 3 15:55:43 2003

Shield ability
QuoteReply
# Sep 05 2003 at 12:17 PM Rating: Good
Dynamis
4 posts
An interesting thing to note, apparently this pet has the warrior ability to shield as well. I've only had it do it once so far, but the message was quite distinct.

I assume it will only shield the caster, but as the pet actually has decent DPS I may use it in groups that I'm not charming for CC

Rylvani
Fennin Ro
▲ Page top
Nice spell
QuoteReply
# Aug 12 2003 at 4:39 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591789926147
Scholar
33 posts
When I can't find a group I like, I enjoy partnering with a druid, beastlord, or necro. This spell comes in handy for added dps in those situations or in small group of healer, tank and I. Without giving it anything, this pet regularly hits twice for high 30s to 55, and bashes as well. Haven't parsed it, but if it does so once per tick on avg - its probably in the 10-15 dps range. Safe added dps in crawls or static camps. If you have trouble getting agro slow, then move up next to the mob and sit. Not slowing? - Tash. Works nearly every time for me.

On Luclin this spell was quite expensive, so I ended up paying 1200p to train up to 170 in research, got the new research book, and made it myself.

Nokei OrHaste
Phantasmist of Dark Summit
Luclin
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Nov 03 2006 at 12:14 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592259821933
5 posts
you resercher this spell??? ok with what pages ???
▲ Page top
want aggro?
QuoteReply
# Aug 28 2003 at 10:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246384115
351 posts
Confirmed, the maximum hit for this pet is 55.

If you can't get aggro to engage the pet by methods above (sitting, tashing), root the mob and stand on top of it. Rooted mobs hit whoever's closest.

If it doesn't work, somebody's standing closer or they broke root by hitting the mob. you probably didn't really want aggro anyway...if they're working so hard to keep aggro off you, you're probably annoying them (and the cleric) by trying to get hit. If you can't get aggro it's probably on purpose.

If/when I work with a pet in a group, I use the root method because it has a big advantage: you can LOSE aggro too, by just backing out; and thus limit your pain to a single round of hits. Tash/slow/stun aggro is not so easily shucked off.
<edit> And sitting guarantees you'll get pounded for max damage, probably doubled.

Also, in a group, be considerate and Berserker Spirit yourself if possible, so the healer doesn't have to watch you so close and put the tanks at risk. If BS isn't stacking with cleric buffs, consider a Rune between fights.

All of these complications are the reason I seldom stick out a pet in a group.
Love to solo with 'em though!


Edited, Thu Aug 28 23:20:08 2003
▲ Page top
note on scroll name
QuoteReply
# Feb 15 2003 at 2:09 PM Rating: Decent

Scholar
41 posts
Note: Scroll name is Zurnaik's Animation. Changes to the above name when its memmed and in your spellbook.
